description: city in and county seat of Marinette County, Wisconsin, United States
description_wiki: Marinette is a city in Marinette County, Wisconsin, United States, and its county seat.

description: independent city in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Norfolk (locally   NOR-fək) is an independent city in the U.S. commonwealth of Virginia.

description_wiki: Philadelphia (  FIL-ə-DEL-fee-ə), colloquially referred to as Philly, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Pennsylvania, and the sixth-most populous city in the United States, with a Census-estimated population of 1,574,281 in July 2025.

description: breakwater in Sussex County, United States of America
description_wiki: The Delaware Breakwater is a set of breakwaters east of Lewes, Delaware on Cape Henlopen that form Lewes Harbor.

description: city in Russia
description_wiki: Omsk is the administrative center and largest city of Omsk Oblast, Russia.

description: city in Maryland, United States
description_wiki: Baltimore ( BAWL-tim-or, locally:  BAWL-dim-OR or  BAWL-mər), also known as Baltimore City, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Maryland.

description: city in Merseyside, England, United Kingdom
description_wiki: Liverpool is a port city and metropolitan borough in Merseyside, England.

description: faction of the Marxist Russian Social Democratic Labour Party; a major faction in the Russian Civil War
description_wiki: The Bolsheviks, led by Vladimir Lenin, were a radical faction of the Marxist Russian Social Democratic Labour Party (RSDLP) which split with the Mensheviks at the Second Party Congress in 1903.

description: municipality in the province of Nova Scotia, Canada
description_wiki: Halifax, officially the Halifax Regional Municipality (HRM), is the capital and most populous municipality of the Canadian province of Nova Scotia, and the most populous municipality in the Atlantic provinces of Eastern Canada.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

ocean between Europe, Africa and the Americas
description_wiki: The Atlantic Ocean is the second largest of the world's five oceanic divisions, with an area of about 85,133,000 square kilometers (32,870,000 sq mi).

description: warship type designed for escort duties against smaller vessels
description_wiki:
In naval terminology, a destroyer is a fast, maneuverable, long-endurance warship intended to escort
larger vessels in a fleet, convoy, or carrier battle group and defend them against a wide range of general threats.

description: British ship
description_wiki: RMS Cedric was an ocean liner owned by the White Star Line.

description: harbour in the New York City area, USA
description_wiki: New York Harbor is a bay that covers all of the Upper Bay.

description: group of lakes in North America
description_wiki: The Great Lakes, also called the Great Lakes of North America, are a series of large interconnected freshwater lakes spanning the Canada–United States border.

description: one of the Great Lakes in North America
description_wiki: Lake Erie ( EER-ee) is the fourth-largest lake by surface area of the five Great Lakes in North America and the eleventh-largest globally.

description: peninsula in Norfolk County, Ontario, Canada
description_wiki: Long Point is a sand spit and medium-sized hamlet on the north shore of Lake Erie, part of Norfolk County in the province of Ontario, Canada.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: city in and county seat of Wayne County, and largest city in State of Michigan, United States
description_wiki: Detroit (  dih-TROYT, locally also  DEE-troyt) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Michigan.

prohibition of alcohol in the United States
description: constitutional ban on alcoholic beverages
description_wiki: The Prohibition era was the period from 1920 to 1933 when the United States prohibited the production, importation, transportation, and sale of alcoholic beverages.
